description: You are a university linear algebra instructor preparing a GeoGebra demonstration for your lecture on eigenvalues and eigenvectors. Create a construction in GeoGebra that visualizes the linear transformation defined by the matrix A = {{2,1},{1,2}} (from Strang's Introduction to Linear Algebra, Example 6.1). The construction must include a unit square with vertices at (0,0), (1,0), (1,1), and (0,1), and its transformed image under matrix A. Include vectors from the origin representing the eigenvectors for each eigenvalue of A, and a text annotation displaying the eigenvalues and the determinant of the matrix. Save the construction as 'linear_transform_eigen.ggb' in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/.
